query: Hi, I tried to top up my Revolut account, but the transaction was declined by my bank. Can you let me know why this happened and how I can fix it?
answer: Your top-up transaction was likely declined by your bank. This could be due to technical issues or a dispute. You can contact your bank for more information on the decline. If you need further assistance, you can tap on the declined transaction in your Revolut app for more details and help.
extracted_context:
[Article 1] Why was my top-up reverted?
## Technical issues
Temporary issues with Revolut or the card issuer's system may cause a card deposit to appear as pending for a prolonged time, and then get reverted. If that happens, tap the deposit in your transactions list for more information and help.

## Your bank disputed the charge
When you add money to your Revolut account using an external card, sometimes the bank may revert the transaction. This is known as a top-up chargeback. If your bank issues a top-up chargeback, the funds will be temporarily blocked on your Revolut account while the transaction is disputed.
- If the bank you transferred money from wins the dispute, the deposit amount will be refunded to the account you topped up from
- If they lose the dispute, the deposit in your Revolut account will be unblocked
For more detailed information and updates on your dispute, contact your bank.

---

[Article 2] Why was my ATM withdrawal declined?
## If you can't see the withdrawal in-app
- Restrictions may have been applied by the external financial institution
- There may be technical issues on the external financial institution's side
Since we have no visibility of the declined withdrawal from our side, we ask that you fill out this formhttps://revolut.com/app-form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-was-my-cash-withdrawal-declined so we can get in touch with the financial institution and resolve the matter as soon as possible.
You can also try to troubleshoot your declined withdrawal by using this link.https://app.revolut.com/form/44f82e23-76fc-44db-859e-8bad0f51a56e?faqEntryPoint=question.why-was-my-cash-withdrawal-declined

## Check the withdrawal in your history
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Select the transaction that was declined to view its details. You'll see the reason for the declined withdrawal in the 'Status' section
- For more help, tap 'Get help' 
If you've been charged incorrectly, you'll see support options after you tap 'Get help'.

## My cash withdrawal was reverted
If an ATM withdrawal transaction is reverted, it means that the transaction was cancelled and the funds were released back to your available balance immediately.
This can happen if the ATM provider decided to cancel a payment, or if they didn't claim the funds within their allocated time.
To find out why this may have happened, contact the ATM provider directly.

---

[Article 3] My card payment was declined
## Why can't I see a declined payment in the app?
- Because of restrictions set by the merchant
- Technical issues on the merchant's side
We can't see the declined payment from our side, but you can fill out this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declinedrevolut://app/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined so that we can reach out to the merchant and resolve the issue as soon as possible.
Alternatively, use this linkhttps://app.revolut.com/form/44f82e23-76fc-44db-859e-8bad0f51a56e?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined to try to identify the issue and find a solution for the declined withdrawal yourself.

## How do I check why a transaction was declined?
- From your home screen, open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list by tapping 'See all' below your most recent transactions
- Tap the declined payment to see the transaction's details
- The reason for the declined payment should be visible in the 'Status' section
- For more support, tap 'Get help'

## What do I do if my card isn't working?
If you're trying to pay with your physical card and the same method (chip and PIN, contactless, or swipe) fails multiple times, check your card for damage and consider ordering a replacement card in-app/help/cards/get-a-card. Fees may apply.

---

[Article 4] Why your card top-ups may be blocked
Card deposits may be blocked because your issuing bank has declined a previous payment using this card, or because you've made multiple unsuccessful attempts to make the payment. These payment blocks can be applied for up to 30 days, due to Visa and Mastercard payment processing rules.

## Reasons for the temporary block
Some common reasons which lead to these payment blocks are:
- Insufficient funds
- Security concerns, like if your card has been reported lost
- Transactions aren't supported by the issuing bank
If you'd like to understand more about these decline reasons, contact your bank or card issuer.

## Add money through other methods
If this happens, you can still use your card with Apple Pay or Google Pay, or try adding money with a different card. For other ways to add money to your account, take a look at the links below:
- Bank transfer/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/receiving-a-bank-transfer
- Internal transfers/help/transfers/internal-transfers/send-or-request-money-from-other-revolut-customers from other Revolut customers
- Easy bank transfers/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/add-money-with-an-easy-bank-transfer

Temporary issues with Revolut or the card issuer's system may cause a card deposit to appear as pending for a prolonged time, and then get reverted. If that happens, tap the deposit in your transactions list for more information and help.

## Your bank disputed the charge
When you add money to your Revolut account using an external card, sometimes the bank may revert the transaction. This is known as a top-up chargeback. If your bank issues a top-up chargeback, the funds will be temporarily blocked on your Revolut account while the transaction is disputed.
- If the bank you transferred money from wins the dispute, the deposit amount will be refunded to the account you topped up from
- If they lose the dispute, the deposit in your Revolut account will be unblocked
For more detailed information and updates on your dispute, contact your bank.

---

[Article 2] Why your card top-ups may be blocked
Card deposits may be blocked because your issuing bank has declined a previous payment using this card, or because you've made multiple unsuccessful attempts to make the payment. These payment blocks can be applied for up to 30 days, due to Visa and Mastercard payment processing rules.

## Reasons for the temporary block
Some common reasons which lead to these payment blocks are:
- Insufficient funds
- Security concerns, like if your card has been reported lost
- Transactions aren't supported by the issuing bank
If you'd like to understand more about these decline reasons, contact your bank or card issuer.

## Add money through other methods
If this happens, you can still use your card with Apple Pay or Google Pay, or try adding money with a different card. For other ways to add money to your account, take a look at the links below:
- Bank transfer/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/receiving-a-bank-transfer
- Internal transfers/help/transfers/internal-transfers/send-or-request-money-from-other-revolut-customers from other Revolut customers
- Easy bank transfers/help/adding-money/add-money-with-a-bank-transfer/add-money-with-an-easy-bank-transfer

---

[Article 3] My card payment was declined
## Why can't I see a declined payment in the app?
- Because of restrictions set by the merchant
- Technical issues on the merchant's side
We can't see the declined payment from our side, but you can fill out this formhttps://app.revolut.com/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declinedrevolut://app/form/13ef84b4-1e3b-48eb-8e8b-0030e7e08adc?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined so that we can reach out to the merchant and resolve the issue as soon as possible.
Alternatively, use this linkhttps://app.revolut.com/form/44f82e23-76fc-44db-859e-8bad0f51a56e?faqEntryPoint=question.why-has-my-card-payment-been-declined to try to identify the issue and find a solution for the declined withdrawal yourself.

## How do I check why a transaction was declined?
- From your home screen, open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list by tapping 'See all' below your most recent transactions
- Tap the declined payment to see the transaction's details
- The reason for the declined payment should be visible in the 'Status' section
- For more support, tap 'Get help'

## What do I do if my card isn't working?
If you're trying to pay with your physical card and the same method (chip and PIN, contactless, or swipe) fails multiple times, check your card for damage and consider ordering a replacement card in-app/help/cards/get-a-card. Fees may apply.

---

[Article 4] Cover for delayed trips
## Delayed Trips
If your trip is delayed due to a reason covered by the policy, you may be entitled to compensation for expenses incurred.
- Metal plan holders can claim for delays of eight hours or more
- Ultra plan holders can claim for delays of four hours or more
You can get up to a maximum of £500 to £100 per hour starting after the minimum delay time (if you have expenses and can provide receipts).

## Missed departure
If you miss your departure for a trip abroad due to a reason covered by the policy, you may be covered for your travel and any extra accommodation costs needed to get to your destination. This cover is up to £200 per night, per insured person, for up to five nights. 
This must be of the same standard as you had originally bought.

## Key exclusions
If you're eligible for cover, you can raise a claim directly with the insurance provider. Learn more in this FAQ/help/insurance-products/insuring-my-travel/how-to-make-a-travel-insurance-claim/making-a-travel-insurance-claim. Your cover may not apply if:
- You can recover the costs from any other source (including if you are refunded with a voucher)
- You didn't check in before the departure time shown on your travel itinerary
- You knew there will be a strike before booking your trip 
- You travel against government advice

## How to check your coverage
This is only a summary of your coverage. Revolut travel insurance is provided through Chubb, not Revolut itself. Chubb is responsible for policy terms, conditions, and claim handling. If you have questions regarding the cover, contact Chubb or read the T&Cs by following these steps:  
- Tap this link or go to 'Home' on the bottom menu, look for the search bar at the top of the screen, and search for 'insurance'
- Then, select 'Travel' and tap 'Documents'

answer:

2. Ensure you authenticated the deposit with your bank using 3-D Secure (3DS).
3. If needed, try again with an Apple Pay or Google Pay deposit.

For more information, you can refer to this article: [Article 4] How to add money with a card.

If you are having trouble adding money via easy bank transfer, it may be due to one of the reasons below:

## Availability or incomplete authorisation

After selecting a bank, you'll be redirected to the bank's app or site to authorise the payment (in some cases via an intermediary partner's screen). If you fail or cancel the payment authorisation, the money will not arrive to your Revolut account.

## External bank errors or rejections
- Most errors encountered during payment authorisation are caused by the external banks' system 
- External bank rejections can also cause your payment to fail. This solely lies at the discretion of your external bank
In these cases, reach out to your external bank or try again later, as we don't have any control over the external bank availability and rejections.

## Other non-technical issues
- Account name mismatch: You can only add money with linked accounts if the name of your external account is the exact same as the one in your Revolut account.
- You can either change the name attached to your Revolut account or use a different bank account which has the correct name attached. To find out how to change the name on your Revolut account, read these FAQs/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details
- Insufficient funds: Double-check your current balance in the external bank account, and add more money if necessary
- If you are requested to input an IBAN before being redirected to your external bank, ensure it's in the correct format and belongs to your external bank account

- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap Add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup

- Set the currency to your card's local currency to avoid exchange fees 
- Select a saved card. If you want to add another card, tap 'Debit card'
- Follow the prompts and tap 'Continue'
If you need to add money in a different currency, you can exchange it in-app after you've topped up. 
We don't support deposits from all cards, countries, and currencies. Learn more in this supported card deposits help article/help/adding-money/with-card/supported-card-deposits, or our card deposit limits and fees FAQ/help/adding-money/card-deposit-limits-and-fees.

- Issuer decline: your bank has declined the transaction based on one of their internal rules, such as exceeding certain limits
- 3-D Secure (3DS) error: you didn't authenticate the deposit with your bank, usually via a notification in their app or with a one-time password via text message. query: Hi there, I've received a request for proof of funds and I'm not quite sure what documents I need to upload. Can you give me a clear list of what's acceptable for this? Thanks!
answer: To provide proof of funds, you can upload documents such as tax return statements, recent payslips, bank transfers, letters from employers, investment statements, property sale contracts, inheritance documents, legal settlement confirmation, gift proof, or gaming win evidence. Ensure the document matches your source of funds.
extracted_context:
[Article 1] Documents for the source of funds verification
Accepted documents can vary depending on your source of income.

## Employment and freelance income
- Tax return statement or tax declaration
- Recent payslip(s) or pension slip(s) issued within the last 12 months
- Bank transfer confirming the receipt of income, clearly showing the employer's name in the 'Sender' or 'Description' field
- Letter from employer confirming salary
- Work contract, dated and signed by employer and employee, stating the duration of employment, the salary and/or additional benefits
- Invoices dated within the last 12 months, clearly showing your full name, address, and VAT number
- Contracts for services provided as a freelancer, dated and signed by both parties, stating the duration of the agreement, payment terms and/or additional benefits

## Income from investments and financial assets
- Dividends statement, certificate, or voucher clearly showing the amount and your position (you must be a director or shareholder)
- Account statement reflecting credit of amount from a particular source
- Statement from investment manager or provider
- Investment certificate or portfolio confirming the sale of shares and withdrawal of money
- Tax declaration

## Property sale or inheritance
- Signed letter from the solicitor
- Copy of contract of sale
- Tax declaration
- Real estate title deed, showing the change of ownership from the previous owner to the new owner, the amount, and the date
- Signed letter from the estate agent
- Grant of probate (with a copy of the will), including the value of the estate
- Account statement showing the receipt of the funds and main account balance
In the case of inheritance, both of the last 2 documents must be provided.

## Other source of funds types
Legal settlement: must provide a court order, written confirmation of settlement signed by the advocate or solicitor, and an account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.
Gifts: proof of transfer from the donor is required, as well as the full name of the donor and relationship.
Gaming wins: either a statement of deposit, win, or withdrawal from the gambling platform or evidence of wins and withdrawals from any platforms or organised tournaments. Also provide a corresponding account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.

---

[Article 2] Accepted proof of address documents
We accept a variety of documents to verify your residential address. All documents must meet the criteria outlined in this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/i-need-to-verify-my-address in our Help Centre.

## Supported bank documents
- Bank statements that include actual bank transactions (including online financial institutions, like Monzo, and Starling)
- Credit card statements
- Any other official correspondence from a bank
- Revolut account statements are not accepted

## Supported residential documents
- Utility bills related to your property, such as electricity, water, gas, council tax, landline telephone, broadband, and internet bills. Mobile service bills (for example, a Vodafone bill) are not accepted
- House purchase agreement or deed
- Lease or rental agreement. These must be signed by both the landlord and tenant

## Supported government documents
- Any letter or notification from the government or recognised public authorities or servants
- Receipts of pension benefits
- Receipts of unemployment benefits
- Receipts of housing benefits

## Other accepted documents
- Letter from a hospital or general practitioner (GP) that includes your address
- ID, passport, driving licence, or residence permit documents that includes your address

---

[Article 3] Source of funds
If we need additional information about your wealth or income, you'll receive an email, a push notification, and see a banner on your in-app 'Home' screen. We'll also let you know if there’s a due date for submitting these details.
Providing this information will ensure your account remains functional and helps combat financial crime.
While you aren't obliged to provide these documents, we'll restrict or close your account as per our T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/?geo-redirect=true if we do not receive them.

## Supported documents
Supported documents depend on your source of ‌funds (investment income, sale of property, etc.). You can check which documents are accepted for your particular case in this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/documents-for-the-source-of-funds-verification.

## Your details
- The name on the document must match the name on your Revolut account
- Make sure you're providing accurate and up-to-date information about your income and wealth

## Document upload
- Update your app to the latest version
- Make sure all documents are clear and readable. Documents that are covered up or cut out of the frame won't be accepted
- Upload PDFs or photos of physical documents. Screenshots aren't accepted

---

[Article 4] How do I provide proof of address?
If we need you to provide proof of address, you'll receive an email, push notification, and see a banner on your in-app home screenhttps://app.revolut.com/accountsrevolut://app/wallet. Simply follow the instructions to upload your document.
Verification typically takes less than 1 hour, but it can take up to 1 business day for some cases.

## Supported document and criteria
You'll need to upload a PDF, or a photo of a physical document. Screenshots are not accepted. Supported documents will be listed on the submission screen.
- The issuing date must be within the last 3 months
- Documents must be issued in Latin alphabet
- Documents must be issued by a government authority, utility company, or financial institution
- The address must be a residential address. Post Office boxes, hotels, and business addresses are not accepted
- The document must not be handwritten. If you're providing a rental/lease agreement, it can be accepted if the 'Template' is printed and only details and signature are handwritten
- You can find a list of accepted proof of address documents in this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/accepted-proof-of-address-documents

## Your details
- The name on the document must match the name on your Revolut account
- Your document must match the address linked to your Revolut account, otherwise you'll need to update your address first

## How to upload the document
Simply follow the email or in-app prompts to upload your proof of address document. Make sure your Revolut app is updated to the latest version.
Your document should be clear and readable. Blurry documents won't be accepted.
For steps on how to update your address in-app, go to this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/how-do-i-change-my-country-of-residence.

query: Hi, I've raised a chargeback dispute for a transaction I didn't recognise a few weeks ago, and I wanted to check the current status and if there's an estimated timeline for resolution. Cheers!
answer:
To check the status of your chargeback dispute and view the estimated resolution timeline, please follow these steps:

1. Open your Revolut mobile app.
2. Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu.
3. Tap 'See all' to open your transactions list.
4. Tap the disputed transaction.
5. View the dispute tracker.

The resolution timeline for disputes can vary depending on the case type. For more details, you can refer to the "Chargeback timeframes" section in the provided help articles.
extracted_context:
[Article 1] Update on my chargeback case
## Can I get an update on my case?
Not to worry, we'll update you during each stage of the dispute process and let you know the expected date for an outcome. We'll also provide an explanation when any decision has been reached via email and in-app.

## How to check the status of a dispute
In the Revolut mobile app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the disputed transaction
- View the dispute tracker

## I need to provide updated information to Revolut
If we need any additional information to support your case, we'll let you know in-app. We'll wait up to a maximum of 15 days to hear back from you. If you fail to reply within this timeframe, we'll proceed to cancel your case.

## Chargeback timeframes
How long you'll likely need to wait for an outcome depends on the case type:
- Fraud cases: within 10 days after you report the matter to us
- ATM cases: up to 45 days after you report the matter to us
- Disputes: up to 120 days after you report the matter to us

---

[Article 2] Dispute process and timelines
After you submit a dispute, we'll review your case and decide whether there's sufficient evidence to initiate a chargeback. A chargeback is a formal process to reverse the transaction through the card network (e.g. Visa, Mastercard).
This process is regulated by strict rules and evidence requirements. 
Up to 85% of disputes are resolved within 5 weeks, but complex cases may take up to 12 weeks. Since the process is regulated, we can't speed this process up. To track your dispute status, tap the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list in your Revolut app.

## Initial assessment
- You fill out a form to initiate the dispute
- Once the transaction is completed, we review your dispute case usually within 8 days, and if there is sufficient evidence, we will proceed to the next stage
- If we need more information, we'll reach out to you
- After this, we'll come to an initial decision. Depending on the type of dispute, we may be obliged to wait a minimum period of 15 days before raising with the merchant, as per card network rules
If your dispute is successful, you'll be refunded by us or the merchant. If your dispute is declined or cancelled, you're not refunded and your case is closed. If we raise your case to the merchant, your case is still open and proceeds to the next step. We'll keep you updated every step of the way.

## Merchant review
If we decide to raise the dispute to the merchant, we reach out to them. The merchant has 30 days to respond for Visa or 45 days for Mastercard transactions.
- If the merchant agrees or doesn't respond, you'll receive a refund for the associated transaction(s)
- If the merchant disagrees, they'll provide supporting documentation and the case will proceed to the next step

## Second assessment
We receive and review the merchant's evidence within 5 days and reach a decision. 
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
- If the evidence is insufficient, the dispute process continues

## Final assessment before resolution
Once the case is raised to the merchant for a second time (if needed), they have 30 days to respond, after which we'll make a final decision.
- If the merchant agrees, you'll receive a refund
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
If it's unresolved and the merchant decides to keep disputing, we may escalate the case to Visa or Mastercard for review, which may take approximately 40 additional days.

---

[Article 3] Dispute a card payment
## Contact the merchant
Most issues like cancellations, refunds, or wrong charges are best resolved directly with the merchant. It’s faster and usually required before submitting a dispute. 
If you still need to dispute the transaction, make sure you gather evidence of the merchant's response, as this can significantly help your case.

## How to dispute a card transaction

- If the merchant disagrees, they'll provide supporting documentation and the case will proceed to the next step

- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
- If the evidence is insufficient, the dispute process continues

- If the merchant agrees, you'll receive a refund
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund

query: Hi, I’d like to know the exact steps to permanently close my Revolut account and withdraw the remaining balance. Can you guide me through the process?
answer:
To permanently close your Revolut account and withdraw the remaining balance, you can follow these steps:

1. Ensure your account meets the closure requirements mentioned in the first article.
2. Open the Revolut app, tap your profile icon, select 'Account,' and then 'Close account.'
3. If your balance is too low for an outbound transfer, top up your account or transfer the remaining funds to another account.
4. If you have a Revolut Pro account, follow the additional steps mentioned in the second article to close it.
5. If you have an investment account, follow the steps in the third article to close it.
6. If you have a Joint account, follow the steps in the fourth article to close it.

Remember to download any statements you may need before closing your account.

answer: To unblock your card after a security check, you need to complete the in-app questionnaire. You can access the questionnaire by following the steps outlined in this article: [Article 1].
extracted_context:
[Article 1] My card payment was declined by the security system
Your transaction may be declined if our security system identifies potential fraud. To keep your money safe, we need you to review the payment by completing our in-app questionnaire.
You can access the questionnaire by going to the home page of your Revolut app and tapping the relevant tile. You can also access the questionnaire through the push notification and email sent to you.

## If your transaction was identified as a potential scam
The merchant will be blocked and all card payments to the merchant will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you choose to unblock the merchant, you can then retry the payment.
If you choose to keep the merchant blocked, all card payments to the merchant and similar merchants will be blocked for the next 7 days. This block can’t be removed during this timeframe.

## If your card is frozen
All payments made with the frozen card will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you mark that the payment was made by you, your card will be unfrozen — you can then retry the payment. If you don't recognise the payment, your card will be permanently terminated. You can order a new card to replace the terminated card.
If you accidentally terminate your card, you can reactivate it: Go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu → tap the card icon in the top-right corner

---

[Article 2] Freeze a card
If you've lost your card, or you suspect it was compromised or notice suspicious transactions, freezing your card can protect your money and account. It prevents unauthorised use and allows us to look into the situation while knowing your account is secure.

## How to freeze a card
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu of the Revolut mobile app
- Tap the card icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the card you wish to freeze
- Tap 'Freeze'
While the card is frozen, any card payments attempted with this card will fail. To unfreeze the card, tap 'Unfreeze' by following the same steps you used to freeze it.

## Alternative steps
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select Security and 'Lost card'
- Select a card and tap 'Freeze'

## Revolut web app steps
- Go to the home screen
- Click the card icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the card you wish to freeze
- Click 'Freeze'
You can also block your card by calling our automated phone line on +44 20 3322 8352. If you need help blocking or unblocking a merchant, read this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/scheduled-payments/how-to-block-and-unblock-a-merchant in our Help Centre.

---

[Article 3] My card has been used fraudulently
## Report a fraudulent transaction and replace your card
If you see that unauthorised charges have been made with your lost or stolen card, report them as fraudulent payments. To do this from your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Go to transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list 
- Tap the relevant fraudulent transaction and 'Get help'
- Select 'Report this payment as fraudulent' and 'Raise claim'
- Follow the prompts to enter the required information
- Tap 'Yes, order a card now' after submitting the claim and replace your card
Raising a claim will terminate your card so that it can no longer be used fraudulently. Once your claim is submitted, you don't need to take any further action. We'll get in touch about the progress of your claim. To temporarily freeze your card, follow the steps outlined in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/ping-other-card-security-settings/freezing-or-blocking-your-card.

---

[Article 4] My card has been swallowed by an ATM
If your card is swallowed by an ATM terminal, freeze your card to prevent any unauthorised transactions. Then, in the app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Choose your card and tap 'Freeze'
Contact the ATM provider and ask for instructions on how to retrieve your card. Most ATMs should display the contact details on the machine.

## Help with a pending transaction
If you tried to withdraw funds, but the card was swallowed, and you see a pending transaction, it should be returned automatically.
You can check the date of the reversal in the 'Status' section of the payment. If the transaction is completed, select the payment from the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and tap 'Get help' to submit a chargeback request.

While you wait for your new card to arrive, you can add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay, and even make payments using your phone. To learn how, visit these FAQs/help/card-payments-withdrawals/getting-started-with-card-payments/paying-with-apple-or-google-pay in our Help Centre.

If the feature is enabled and the issue persists, check if the problem is being caused on the merchant’s side by trying to pay using another payment terminal.

## My card isn't working for chip and PIN payments
The reason why your card isn’t working should be visible in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. Some other reasons could be:
- PIN tries exceeded
- Card blocked due to location-based security
- Spending limit reached

## Where do I submit my proof of identity?
If we need you to provide proof of identity, you'll get an email, push notification, and a banner on your home screen. Simply tap any notification we send you about verifying your identity, then follow the steps provided to send your proof of identity.

## I don't have the required document
- All supported document types will be listed on the in-app submission screen

## My document has been rejected
To pass the verification, your identity document must meet the following criteria:
- It's a supported identity document type
- It isn't handwritten
- It isn't expired, damaged, or hard to read
- The personal information on the document matches the information in your Revolut account
If you have a duplicate account, visit this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/log-in-issues/i-think-i-have-a-duplicate-account for more information.

- Select the transaction that was declined to view its details. You'll see the reason for the declined withdrawal in the 'Status' section
- For more help, tap 'Get help' 
If you've been charged incorrectly, you'll see support options after you tap 'Get help'.

## My cash withdrawal was reverted
If an ATM withdrawal transaction is reverted, it means that the transaction was cancelled and the funds were released back to your available balance immediately.
This can happen if the ATM provider decided to cancel a payment, or if they didn't claim the funds within their allocated time.
To find out why this may have happened, contact the ATM provider directly.

[Article 3] How to transfer funds to Revolut
## Timeframes
If you're expecting a bank transfer to your Revolut account, we usually can't track its progress until it appears in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. How long a transfer will take to reach your account depends on the sending bank, so you'll need to reach out to the sending bank for their timeframes.
Because this bank handles initiating the transfer, we can't influence its status or processing before it reaches us. However, you can get an estimated arrival date by using our in-app transfer tracker, which you can access via this link.
For any delays, check with the sending bank for updates. Once the transfer arrives, it will be instantly accessible, and you'll receive an automatic notification. For general transfer timeframes, check this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/timeframes-for-transfers. 

## How to receive a bank transfer
To receive a bank transfer, you must share your bank details with the sender so that they can initiate the transfer. To learn how to find these details in-app, visit this article/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/how-to-receive-money-from-another-bank/what-account-details-should-i-use-to-transfer-money-to-my-revolut-account/what-account-details-are-available-for-me.
We don't support transfers from some countries and currencies. You can find out more about unsupported transfers on this page/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/which-countries-and-currencies-are-supported-to-receive-money/which-countries-are-not-supported-for-inbound-transfers.

## Limits and fees
There are no limits for receiving a bank transfer. Usually, no additional steps are needed to receive large amounts of money. If we do need additional information, we'll reach out to you via email.
Revolut doesn't charge any fees for receiving a bank transfer. Intermediary banks may charge fees. You can learn more about intermediary fees in this FAQ/help/transfers/bank-transfers/which-fees-are-charged-for-a-bank-transfer.

## Difficulty receiving a bank transfer
If there are delays, contact the sending bank for more updates, ask for a transfer confirmation document or a GPI tracker in case of international SWIFT transfers, and submit it to us.
If our tracker considers your transfer to be taking longer than usual, our team will review it. If it's still within the expected timeframe, wait until the timeframe passes.
If your transfer didn't arrive within the expected timeframe, you can find the next steps in this FAQ/help/transfers/inbound-transfers/having-an-issue-with-receiving-money/what-should-i-do-if-a-transfer-doesn-t-arrive-to-my-revolut-account.

answer:
Bonjour! Il semble que votre banque ait refusé le transfert d'argent vers votre compte Revolut. Voici quelques raisons possibles et des étapes pour résoudre le problème :

1. Vérifiez si votre banque est prise en charge pour le transfert facile. Assurez-vous de suivre les étapes d'autorisation complète décrites dans l'article 1.

2. Assurez-vous que le nom de votre compte externe correspond exactement à celui de votre compte Revolut. Vous pouvez également vérifier s'il y a suffisamment de fonds dans votre compte externe.

3. Si vous essayez d'ajouter de l'argent avec une carte, suivez les étapes de l'article 3 pour ajouter de l'argent avec une carte.

Si ces étapes ne résolvent pas le problème, je vous recommande de contacter votre banque pour plus d'assistance.

[Article 1] Difficulty adding money with an easy bank transfer
If you are having trouble adding money via easy bank transfer, it may be due to one of the reasons below:

## Availability or incomplete authorisation
We only offer this feature from a certain list of local banks that support this type of payment. The list of banks offered depends on your Revolut account country, and not your nationality.
After selecting a bank, you'll be redirected to the bank's app or site to authorise the payment (in some cases via an intermediary partner's screen). If you fail or cancel the payment authorisation, the money will not arrive to your Revolut account.

## External bank errors or rejections
- Most errors encountered during payment authorisation are caused by the external banks' system 
- External bank rejections can also cause your payment to fail. This solely lies at the discretion of your external bank
In these cases, reach out to your external bank or try again later, as we don't have any control over the external bank availability and rejections.

## Other non-technical issues
- Account name mismatch: You can only add money with linked accounts if the name of your external account is the exact same as the one in your Revolut account.
- You can either change the name attached to your Revolut account or use a different bank account which has the correct name attached. To find out how to change the name on your Revolut account, read these FAQs/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/update-personal-details
- Insufficient funds: Double-check your current balance in the external bank account, and add more money if necessary
- If you are requested to input an IBAN before being redirected to your external bank, ensure it's in the correct format and belongs to your external bank account

Additionally,‌ the recipient may have blocked you, which could be the reason the transfer isn't going through.

## Internal transfers disabled
If internal transfers are disabled, your transfers to or from other Revolut customers won't work. Check if you and the recipient have the 'Make me discoverable' option turned on. To do this:

- Select Privacyhttps://app.revolut.com/settings/security/privacyrevolut://app/privacy

Revolut must complete necessary checks, by law, to validate the security of all transfers. If your transfer doesn't meet the requirements of this check, it will be declined.

If you don't have enough funds to complete a transfer, you won't be able to fulfil a request or send a new transfer. If that's the case, you'll need to add moneyhttps://app.revolut.com/accounts/topuprevolut://app/topup to your account.

answer: Pour prouver la source de vos fonds, vous devrez télécharger des documents tels que des relevés bancaires, des déclarations fiscales, des contrats de travail, des certificats d'investissement, des lettres de l'employeur, des contrats de vente immobilière, des lettres du notaire, des preuves de transfert de dons, des relevés de jeux de hasard, etc. Assurez-vous que les documents téléchargés sont clairs, lisibles et contiennent toutes les informations nécessaires.

[Article 1] Documents for the source of funds verification
Accepted documents can vary depending on your source of income.

## Employment and freelance income
- Tax return statement or tax declaration
- Recent payslip(s) or pension slip(s) issued within the last 12 months
- Bank transfer confirming the receipt of income, clearly showing the employer's name in the 'Sender' or 'Description' field
- Letter from employer confirming salary
- Work contract, dated and signed by employer and employee, stating the duration of employment, the salary and/or additional benefits
- Invoices dated within the last 12 months, clearly showing your full name, address, and VAT number
- Contracts for services provided as a freelancer, dated and signed by both parties, stating the duration of the agreement, payment terms and/or additional benefits

## Income from investments and financial assets
- Dividends statement, certificate, or voucher clearly showing the amount and your position (you must be a director or shareholder)
- Account statement reflecting credit of amount from a particular source
- Statement from investment manager or provider
- Investment certificate or portfolio confirming the sale of shares and withdrawal of money
- Tax declaration

## Property sale or inheritance
- Signed letter from the solicitor
- Copy of contract of sale
- Tax declaration
- Real estate title deed, showing the change of ownership from the previous owner to the new owner, the amount, and the date
- Signed letter from the estate agent
- Grant of probate (with a copy of the will), including the value of the estate
- Account statement showing the receipt of the funds and main account balance
In the case of inheritance, both of the last 2 documents must be provided.

## Other source of funds types
Legal settlement: must provide a court order, written confirmation of settlement signed by the advocate or solicitor, and an account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.
Gifts: proof of transfer from the donor is required, as well as the full name of the donor and relationship.
Gaming wins: either a statement of deposit, win, or withdrawal from the gambling platform or evidence of wins and withdrawals from any platforms or organised tournaments. Also provide a corresponding account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.

---

- International bank account number (IBAN) and account numbers for all relevant accounts

Providing this information will ensure your account remains functional and helps combat financial crime.
While you aren't obliged to provide these documents, we'll restrict or close your account as per our T&Cshttps://www.revolut.com/legal/terms/?geo-redirect=true if we do not receive them.

## Supported documents
Supported documents depend on your source of ‌funds (investment income, sale of property, etc.). You can check which documents are accepted for your particular case in this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/documents-for-the-source-of-funds-verification.

## Your details
- The name on the document must match the name on your Revolut account
- Make sure you're providing accurate and up-to-date information about your income and wealth

## Document upload
- Update your app to the latest version
- Make sure all documents are clear and readable. Documents that are covered up or cut out of the frame won't be accepted
- Upload PDFs or photos of physical documents. Screenshots aren't accepted

---

[Article 2] Documents for the source of funds verification
Accepted documents can vary depending on your source of income.

## Employment and freelance income
- Tax return statement or tax declaration
- Recent payslip(s) or pension slip(s) issued within the last 12 months
- Bank transfer confirming the receipt of income, clearly showing the employer's name in the 'Sender' or 'Description' field
- Letter from employer confirming salary
- Work contract, dated and signed by employer and employee, stating the duration of employment, the salary and/or additional benefits
- Invoices dated within the last 12 months, clearly showing your full name, address, and VAT number
- Contracts for services provided as a freelancer, dated and signed by both parties, stating the duration of the agreement, payment terms and/or additional benefits

## Income from investments and financial assets
- Dividends statement, certificate, or voucher clearly showing the amount and your position (you must be a director or shareholder)
- Account statement reflecting credit of amount from a particular source
- Statement from investment manager or provider
- Investment certificate or portfolio confirming the sale of shares and withdrawal of money
- Tax declaration

## Property sale or inheritance
- Signed letter from the solicitor
- Copy of contract of sale
- Tax declaration
- Real estate title deed, showing the change of ownership from the previous owner to the new owner, the amount, and the date
- Signed letter from the estate agent
- Grant of probate (with a copy of the will), including the value of the estate
- Account statement showing the receipt of the funds and main account balance
In the case of inheritance, both of the last 2 documents must be provided.

## Other source of funds types
Legal settlement: must provide a court order, written confirmation of settlement signed by the advocate or solicitor, and an account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.
Gifts: proof of transfer from the donor is required, as well as the full name of the donor and relationship.
Gaming wins: either a statement of deposit, win, or withdrawal from the gambling platform or evidence of wins and withdrawals from any platforms or organised tournaments. Also provide a corresponding account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.

---

[Article 3] I was asked for additional information about my transaction
To ensure the safety of your account and maintain compliance, we may need to request additional information about a transaction — for example, evidence regarding a transaction or verification of your identity. These measures are part of our commitment to secure financial practices.

## How do I submit the information?
- Follow the instructions you see in-app to verify your identity and submit what's required
- Make sure that the documents you upload are clear, readable, and contain all the necessary details as outlined in the verification instructions

---

[Article 4] Why are you asking me for documents to open or continue using Revolut trading?
Occasionally, we may need you to provide additional documentation or re-verify a few documents and information. We try to keep these requests to a minimum, but we need all the necessary information, for regulatory reasons. If we do need more documents from you, we'll make the experience as straightforward for you as possible, in-app.
If you don't give us the necessary information we need, we may have to restrict your trading account until we receive this information, or we'll have to close it permanently, if we don't get these documents on time.

[Article 3] Documents needed for an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim
When submitting an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim, our insurance provider will request some documents to confirm your eligibility for cover. 
You must provide the following when submitting a claim for Ultra trip and event cancellation protection:
- Proof that you paid for the trip or event with your Revolut account
- Proof of cancellation at least one day before the trip or event and any charges
- Original receipts and invoices
If you cancel your trip for a specific reason, you need to provide additional supporting documents. Some examples include:
- Serious injury or sickness: a medical certificate with the date and details of the accident or illness
- Contagious disease: a medical certificate showing quarantine is required, or a copy of the test results
- Death: a copy of the death certificate
- Quarantine: a copy of the official document that places you or your travel companion in quarantine due to an epidemic or pandemic
- Childbirth: a copy of the birth certificate
- Redundancy: a copy of your company's redundancy notice, proof of your employment contract, and the exact start date of your employment
- Traffic accident: a copy of the breakdown, towing invoice, or report, and a medical report if any medical attention was needed
- Theft of identity papers or tickets: a copy of the police report and any other documents showing your efforts to replace the stolen items

## Important things to remember
- File types: Accepted formats are GIF, JPEG, PDF, and PNG. If one format doesn't work, try another from the list
- Max documents accepted: A maximum of 3 documents can be processed when submitting your claim, so merge files if you have more than this to avoid any problems

---

[Article 4] Download your account confirmation
## Getting your document
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Documents & statements'
- Choose 'Personal'
- Tap 'Account confirmation'

## This document includes:
- Account holder details (name, date of birth, email address, address)
- Account status
- Opening date
- Wallet reference
- Bank identifier code (BIC) and sort code
- International bank account number (IBAN) and account numbers for all relevant accounts
We don't issue physical copies of documents or provide stamps on requested documents — all statements and confirmation documents are electronically generated.

## Supported document and criteria
You'll need to upload a PDF, or a photo of a physical document. Screenshots are not accepted. Supported documents will be listed on the submission screen.
- The issuing date must be within the last 3 months
- Documents must be issued in Latin alphabet
- Documents must be issued by a government authority, utility company, or financial institution
- The address must be a residential address. Post Office boxes, hotels, and business addresses are not accepted
- The document must not be handwritten. If you're providing a rental/lease agreement, it can be accepted if the 'Template' is printed and only details and signature are handwritten
- You can find a list of accepted proof of address documents in this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/accepted-proof-of-address-documents

## Your details
- The name on the document must match the name on your Revolut account
- Your document must match the address linked to your Revolut account, otherwise you'll need to update your address first

Simply follow the email or in-app prompts to upload your proof of address document. Make sure your Revolut app is updated to the latest version.
Your document should be clear and readable. Blurry documents won't be accepted.
For steps on how to update your address in-app, go to this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/profile-settings/how-do-i-change-my-country-of-residence.

[Article 4] Verify my identity
## Where do I submit my proof of identity?
If we need you to provide proof of identity, you'll get an email, push notification, and a banner on your Home screen in-app. To submit, simply tap any notification we send you about your identity checks.
If you don't see any prompt for this information in-app, check your emails as we may already have everything we need. If you're having difficulty with identity verification, visit this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/im-unable-to-verify-my-identity in our Help Centre.

## How long do I have to submit my proof of identity?
We’ll give you a deadline to send the new document by. Your account will keep working until this deadline, but afterwards it may be temporarily restricted until you can provide valid proof of identity.

## Guidelines
- The types of document we accept are listed on the submission screen in-app
- Your documents must be up-to-date
- The name on your document must match the name on your Revolut account
- If your nationality is different from your current residential address, you may be asked for proof of address/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/i-need-to-verify-my-address

## Document upload tips
- Update your Revolut app to the latest version
- Make sure your document is clear and easy to read
- Capture the entire document. Don't fold, crop, or exclude any part of it
- Upload a photo of the original, physical document
- We can't accept pictures of copies, scans, or device screens

query: Bonjour, j'ai essayé de soumettre un document pour vérifier mon adresse, mais il a été rejeté. Pourriez-vous, s'il vous plaît, m'indiquer quel format est accepté et pourquoi mon document n'était pas bon ? Merci d'avance pour votre aide.
answer:
Bonjour! Pour vérifier votre adresse avec succès, assurez-vous de soumettre un document qui respecte les critères suivants :
- Le document doit être un PDF ou une photo d'un document physique
- La date d'émission doit être dans les 3 derniers mois
- Le document doit être émis par une autorité gouvernementale, une entreprise de services publics ou une institution financière
- L'adresse doit être une adresse résidentielle
- Le document ne doit pas être manuscrit
- Assurez-vous que le nom sur le document correspond au nom sur votre compte Revolut

Si votre document a été rejeté, c'était probablement parce qu'il ne respectait pas l'un de ces critères.

- Contagious disease: a medical certificate showing quarantine is required, or a copy of the test results
- Death: a copy of the death certificate

- Childbirth: a copy of the birth certificate

[Article 1] Update on my chargeback case
## Can I get an update on my case?
Not to worry, we'll update you during each stage of the dispute process and let you know the expected date for an outcome. We'll also provide an explanation when any decision has been reached via email and in-app.

## How to check the status of a dispute
In the Revolut mobile app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the disputed transaction
- View the dispute tracker

## I need to provide updated information to Revolut
If we need any additional information to support your case, we'll let you know in-app. We'll wait up to a maximum of 15 days to hear back from you. If you fail to reply within this timeframe, we'll proceed to cancel your case.

## Chargeback timeframes
How long you'll likely need to wait for an outcome depends on the case type:
- Fraud cases: within 10 days after you report the matter to us
- ATM cases: up to 45 days after you report the matter to us
- Disputes: up to 120 days after you report the matter to us

---

[Article 2] Dispute process and timelines
After you submit a dispute, we'll review your case and decide whether there's sufficient evidence to initiate a chargeback. A chargeback is a formal process to reverse the transaction through the card network (e.g. Visa, Mastercard).
This process is regulated by strict rules and evidence requirements. 
Up to 85% of disputes are resolved within 5 weeks, but complex cases may take up to 12 weeks. Since the process is regulated, we can't speed this process up. To track your dispute status, tap the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list in your Revolut app.

## Initial assessment
- You fill out a form to initiate the dispute
- Once the transaction is completed, we review your dispute case usually within 8 days, and if there is sufficient evidence, we will proceed to the next stage
- If we need more information, we'll reach out to you
- After this, we'll come to an initial decision. Depending on the type of dispute, we may be obliged to wait a minimum period of 15 days before raising with the merchant, as per card network rules
If your dispute is successful, you'll be refunded by us or the merchant. If your dispute is declined or cancelled, you're not refunded and your case is closed. If we raise your case to the merchant, your case is still open and proceeds to the next step. We'll keep you updated every step of the way.

## Merchant review
If we decide to raise the dispute to the merchant, we reach out to them. The merchant has 30 days to respond for Visa or 45 days for Mastercard transactions.
- If the merchant agrees or doesn't respond, you'll receive a refund for the associated transaction(s)
- If the merchant disagrees, they'll provide supporting documentation and the case will proceed to the next step

## Second assessment
We receive and review the merchant's evidence within 5 days and reach a decision. 
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
- If the evidence is insufficient, the dispute process continues

## Final assessment before resolution
Once the case is raised to the merchant for a second time (if needed), they have 30 days to respond, after which we'll make a final decision.
- If the merchant agrees, you'll receive a refund
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
If it's unresolved and the merchant decides to keep disputing, we may escalate the case to Visa or Mastercard for review, which may take approximately 40 additional days.

---

[Article 3] Dispute a card payment
## Contact the merchant
Most issues like cancellations, refunds, or wrong charges are best resolved directly with the merchant. It’s faster and usually required before submitting a dispute. 
If you still need to dispute the transaction, make sure you gather evidence of the merchant's response, as this can significantly help your case.

## How to dispute a card transaction
If your direct contact with the merchant is unsuccessful, follow these steps to dispute the card transaction in your Revolut app:
- Tap the transaction in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. If you have multiple to report, you'll be able to add them in the form later
- Tap 'Get help'
- Select an issue
- Complete and submit the form
We'll review your case and get back to you with updates. You can also track the progress of your dispute in-app by tapping the disputed transaction on the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. Learn more about the disputes process and timeliness in this FAQ.

---

[Article 4] How to cancel a dispute
You can only cancel a dispute if we haven't yet shared the case with the merchant.

## To check if you can cancel a dispute:
- From your home screen, tap 'See all' to open your transactions list
- Tap the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- 'Cancel' will appear at the top of the dispute tracker

query: Bonjour, s'il vous plaît, pourriez-vous m'expliquer comment faire pour fermer mon compte Revolut et retirer le solde restant ? Merci d'avance pour votre aide.
answer:
To close your Revolut account and withdraw the remaining balance, follow these steps:

1. Assurez-vous que tous vos comptes, Pockets et comptes de devises ont un solde de zéro.
2. Assurez-vous de ne pas avoir de transactions en attente.
3. Assurez-vous d'avoir la dernière version de l'application Revolut installée.
4. Baissez votre plan au forfait Standard.
5. Fermez votre compte d'investissement.
6. Assurez-vous de ne pas avoir d'abonnements actifs ou de demandes de rétrofacturation.
7. Fermez tout compte Kids & Teens actif.
8. Assurez-vous de ne pas avoir d'assurances actives via Revolut.

Ensuite, dans l'application Revolut :
- Appuyez sur votre icône de profil dans le coin supérieur gauche.
- Sélectionnez 'Compte' et 'Fermer le compte'.

Si votre solde est trop bas pour un virement sortant, suivez les étapes fournies dans l'article.

extracted_context:
[Article 1] My card payment was declined by the security system
Your transaction may be declined if our security system identifies potential fraud. To keep your money safe, we need you to review the payment by completing our in-app questionnaire.
You can access the questionnaire by going to the home page of your Revolut app and tapping the relevant tile. You can also access the questionnaire through the push notification and email sent to you.

## If your transaction was identified as a potential scam
The merchant will be blocked and all card payments to the merchant will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you choose to unblock the merchant, you can then retry the payment.
If you choose to keep the merchant blocked, all card payments to the merchant and similar merchants will be blocked for the next 7 days. This block can’t be removed during this timeframe.

## If your card is frozen
All payments made with the frozen card will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you mark that the payment was made by you, your card will be unfrozen — you can then retry the payment. If you don't recognise the payment, your card will be permanently terminated. You can order a new card to replace the terminated card.
If you accidentally terminate your card, you can reactivate it: Go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu → tap the card icon in the top-right corner

---

[Article 2] My card has been used fraudulently
## Report a fraudulent transaction and replace your card
If you see that unauthorised charges have been made with your lost or stolen card, report them as fraudulent payments. To do this from your Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Go to transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list 
- Tap the relevant fraudulent transaction and 'Get help'
- Select 'Report this payment as fraudulent' and 'Raise claim'
- Follow the prompts to enter the required information
- Tap 'Yes, order a card now' after submitting the claim and replace your card
Raising a claim will terminate your card so that it can no longer be used fraudulently. Once your claim is submitted, you don't need to take any further action. We'll get in touch about the progress of your claim. To temporarily freeze your card, follow the steps outlined in this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/ping-other-card-security-settings/freezing-or-blocking-your-card.

---

[Article 3] Why your card top-ups may be blocked
Card deposits may be blocked because your issuing bank has declined a previous payment using this card, or because you've made multiple unsuccessful attempts to make the payment. These payment blocks can be applied for up to 30 days, due to Visa and Mastercard payment processing rules.

## Reasons for the temporary block
Some common reasons which lead to these payment blocks are:
- Insufficient funds

- Insufficient funds

If you see that unauthorised charges have been made with your lost or stolen card, report them as fraudulent payments. To do this from your Revolut app:

- Go to transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list 
- Tap the relevant fraudulent transaction and 'Get help'

If your card is swallowed by an ATM terminal, freeze your card to prevent any unauthorised transactions. Then, in the app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Tap the cards icon in the top-right corner
- Choose your card and tap 'Freeze'
Contact the ATM provider and ask for instructions on how to retrieve your card. Most ATMs should display the contact details on the machine.

If you tried to withdraw funds, but the card was swallowed, and you see a pending transaction, it should be returned automatically.

[Article 3] The name on my card is incorrect
The name on your card follows specific rules due to a 20-character limit (including spaces). If your full name exceeds this limit, names are shortened as follows:
- First name and last surname. Middle names will be shortened
- Middle names may be removed if necessary
- First name may be shortened to an initial if necessary
- If all else fails, only the last surname will be printed
- Special characters (e.g. umlauts, accents) are replaced with standard Latin equivalents. For example: Müller will appear as Muller

## Why can't my card have non-Latin characters?
Revolut cards only support Latin characters. Special characters, such as Ś/ę/Ą, are replaced with their closest Latin equivalent (S/e/A).
It's not possible to customise the name on your card. To change the name associated with your account, go to your profile and tap Personal details. If you wish to proceed with the name change, you'll need to provide a document proving that the updated name is your legal name.

---

[Article 4] Documents needed for an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim
When submitting an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim, our insurance provider will request some documents to confirm your eligibility for cover. 
You must provide the following when submitting a claim for Ultra trip and event cancellation protection:
- Proof that you paid for the trip or event with your Revolut account
- Proof of cancellation at least one day before the trip or event and any charges
- Original receipts and invoices
If you cancel your trip for a specific reason, you need to provide additional supporting documents. Some examples include:
- Serious injury or sickness: a medical certificate with the date and details of the accident or illness
- Contagious disease: a medical certificate showing quarantine is required, or a copy of the test results
- Death: a copy of the death certificate
- Quarantine: a copy of the official document that places you or your travel companion in quarantine due to an epidemic or pandemic
- Childbirth: a copy of the birth certificate
- Redundancy: a copy of your company's redundancy notice, proof of your employment contract, and the exact start date of your employment
- Traffic accident: a copy of the breakdown, towing invoice, or report, and a medical report if any medical attention was needed
- Theft of identity papers or tickets: a copy of the police report and any other documents showing your efforts to replace the stolen items

## Important things to remember
- File types: Accepted formats are GIF, JPEG, PDF, and PNG. If one format doesn't work, try another from the list
- Max documents accepted: A maximum of 3 documents can be processed when submitting your claim, so merge files if you have more than this to avoid any problems

[Article 1] Documents for the source of funds verification
Accepted documents can vary depending on your source of income.

## Employment and freelance income
- Tax return statement or tax declaration
- Recent payslip(s) or pension slip(s) issued within the last 12 months
- Bank transfer confirming the receipt of income, clearly showing the employer's name in the 'Sender' or 'Description' field
- Letter from employer confirming salary
- Work contract, dated and signed by employer and employee, stating the duration of employment, the salary and/or additional benefits
- Invoices dated within the last 12 months, clearly showing your full name, address, and VAT number
- Contracts for services provided as a freelancer, dated and signed by both parties, stating the duration of the agreement, payment terms and/or additional benefits

## Income from investments and financial assets
- Dividends statement, certificate, or voucher clearly showing the amount and your position (you must be a director or shareholder)
- Account statement reflecting credit of amount from a particular source
- Statement from investment manager or provider
- Investment certificate or portfolio confirming the sale of shares and withdrawal of money
- Tax declaration

## Property sale or inheritance
- Signed letter from the solicitor
- Copy of contract of sale
- Tax declaration
- Real estate title deed, showing the change of ownership from the previous owner to the new owner, the amount, and the date
- Signed letter from the estate agent
- Grant of probate (with a copy of the will), including the value of the estate
- Account statement showing the receipt of the funds and main account balance
In the case of inheritance, both of the last 2 documents must be provided.

## Other source of funds types
Legal settlement: must provide a court order, written confirmation of settlement signed by the advocate or solicitor, and an account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.
Gifts: proof of transfer from the donor is required, as well as the full name of the donor and relationship.
Gaming wins: either a statement of deposit, win, or withdrawal from the gambling platform or evidence of wins and withdrawals from any platforms or organised tournaments. Also provide a corresponding account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.

- Upload PDFs or photos of physical documents. Screenshots aren't accepted

[Article 3] Why are you asking me for documents to open or continue using Revolut trading?
Occasionally, we may need you to provide additional documentation or re-verify a few documents and information. We try to keep these requests to a minimum, but we need all the necessary information, for regulatory reasons. If we do need more documents from you, we'll make the experience as straightforward for you as possible, in-app.
If you don't give us the necessary information we need, we may have to restrict your trading account until we receive this information, or we'll have to close it permanently, if we don't get these documents on time.

---

[Article 4] Documents needed for an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim
When submitting an Ultra trip and event cancellation protection claim, our insurance provider will request some documents to confirm your eligibility for cover. 
You must provide the following when submitting a claim for Ultra trip and event cancellation protection:
- Proof that you paid for the trip or event with your Revolut account
- Proof of cancellation at least one day before the trip or event and any charges
- Original receipts and invoices
If you cancel your trip for a specific reason, you need to provide additional supporting documents. Some examples include:
- Serious injury or sickness: a medical certificate with the date and details of the accident or illness
- Contagious disease: a medical certificate showing quarantine is required, or a copy of the test results
- Death: a copy of the death certificate
- Quarantine: a copy of the official document that places you or your travel companion in quarantine due to an epidemic or pandemic
- Childbirth: a copy of the birth certificate
- Redundancy: a copy of your company's redundancy notice, proof of your employment contract, and the exact start date of your employment
- Traffic accident: a copy of the breakdown, towing invoice, or report, and a medical report if any medical attention was needed
- Theft of identity papers or tickets: a copy of the police report and any other documents showing your efforts to replace the stolen items

## Important things to remember
- File types: Accepted formats are GIF, JPEG, PDF, and PNG. If one format doesn't work, try another from the list
- Max documents accepted: A maximum of 3 documents can be processed when submitting your claim, so merge files if you have more than this to avoid any problems

- Upload PDFs or photos of physical documents. Screenshots aren't accepted

[Article 2] Documents for the source of funds verification
Accepted documents can vary depending on your source of income.

## Employment and freelance income
- Tax return statement or tax declaration
- Recent payslip(s) or pension slip(s) issued within the last 12 months
- Bank transfer confirming the receipt of income, clearly showing the employer's name in the 'Sender' or 'Description' field
- Letter from employer confirming salary
- Work contract, dated and signed by employer and employee, stating the duration of employment, the salary and/or additional benefits
- Invoices dated within the last 12 months, clearly showing your full name, address, and VAT number
- Contracts for services provided as a freelancer, dated and signed by both parties, stating the duration of the agreement, payment terms and/or additional benefits

## Income from investments and financial assets
- Dividends statement, certificate, or voucher clearly showing the amount and your position (you must be a director or shareholder)
- Account statement reflecting credit of amount from a particular source
- Statement from investment manager or provider
- Investment certificate or portfolio confirming the sale of shares and withdrawal of money
- Tax declaration

## Property sale or inheritance
- Signed letter from the solicitor
- Copy of contract of sale
- Tax declaration
- Real estate title deed, showing the change of ownership from the previous owner to the new owner, the amount, and the date
- Signed letter from the estate agent
- Grant of probate (with a copy of the will), including the value of the estate
- Account statement showing the receipt of the funds and main account balance
In the case of inheritance, both of the last 2 documents must be provided.

## Other source of funds types
Legal settlement: must provide a court order, written confirmation of settlement signed by the advocate or solicitor, and an account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.
Gifts: proof of transfer from the donor is required, as well as the full name of the donor and relationship.
Gaming wins: either a statement of deposit, win, or withdrawal from the gambling platform or evidence of wins and withdrawals from any platforms or organised tournaments. Also provide a corresponding account statement clearly showing the receipt of funds and main account balance.

[Article 2] I sent a transfer to the wrong Revolut customer
Transfers between Revolut customers are usually instant. Once the transfer is processed, we're not able to cancel it. If you sent money to the wrong Revolut customer, you'll need to ask the recipient to return the money to you as soon as possible.
To do this, reach out to them via the chat between you both in the Revolut app. This chat is created once you send them money. To use this chat: 
- On the bottom menu, go to Payments
- Select the person you've sent money to
- A chat window will open, and you'll be able to type your message to the recipient of the payment there

## The recipient isn't answering my messages
If the recipient isn't responding, you can contact our customer support team and we'll try to reach out to them on your behalf. However, we can't guarantee they will respond or agree to return the funds. Without the recipient's consent, we're unable to retrieve the money.

---

[Article 3] Problems with a refund
## Refund is 'Waiting for funds'
A refund may take up to 5 days to be processed and credited to your account from the moment it's initiated by the merchant. The funds will appear in the Revolut app once the merchant completes the transaction on their side.
Once we're notified, the refund will appear in your upcoming payments in your Revolut app. You can view it at the top of your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. If the refund is 'Waiting for funds', it means that the merchant is processing it, and it should be completed by the due date.
To view the due date, tap the transaction in your 'Upcoming payments' or 'Transactions list'.

## Refund is declined or reverted
If your refund was declined or reverted, reach out to the merchant and request them to process the refund again.

## Refunds on inactive or single-use cards, or a closed account
These refunds will be processed as normal, even if you used an inactive or single-use card. If the merchant requests a card, let them know that the card is no longer active and provide them with active Revolut card details.

## I received less than expected
If your refund amount is less than expected, contact the merchant directly and clarify it with them. If they aren't responding, you can raise a dispute in-app. To learn how, see this FAQ/help/card-payments-withdrawals/refunds/dispute-a-card-transaction.

---

[Article 4] Why is my incoming transfer pending?
A 'pending' status means the transfer is with us, but we need to complete some final checks before releasing the funds to your account. Common reasons for a pending transfer:
- We're waiting for required information from the sending bank
- The transfer needs to be reviewed to meet regulatory requirements
Pending transfers are usually processed within 3 business days. You should wait up to 5 business days before reporting the transfer as delayed or seeking support. If the transfer is still pending after this time, please contact our support team for help.

## My transfer was reverted
A 'reverted' status means we were unable to process the transfer, and the funds have been sent back to the sender's account. Common reasons for a reverted transfer:
- The transfer details were incorrect or incomplete (e.g. a missing reference number)
- We requested missing personal information for your Revolut account and didn't receive it in time

## What happens next?
The funds will automatically be returned to the sender, but the timeframe depends on the transfer type. Revolut cannot speed up this process as it depends on the sending bank and any intermediary banks.
- Domestic transfers: should arrive back in the sender's account within 5 business days
- International (SWIFT) transfers: can take up to 15 to 20 business days due to the multiple intermediary banks involved
The sender should contact their bank if the funds don't arrive within these timeframes. Once they receive the money, they can attempt to send you the transfer again using the correct details.
