[Article 1] My card payment was declined by the security system
Your transaction may be declined if our security system identifies potential fraud. To keep your money safe, we need you to review the payment by completing our in-app questionnaire.
You can access the questionnaire by going to the home page of your Revolut app and tapping the relevant tile. You can also access the questionnaire through the push notification and email sent to you.

## If your transaction was identified as a potential scam
The merchant will be blocked and all card payments to the merchant will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you choose to unblock the merchant, you can then retry the payment.
If you choose to keep the merchant blocked, all card payments to the merchant and similar merchants will be blocked for the next 7 days. This block can’t be removed during this timeframe.

## If your card is frozen
All payments made with the frozen card will be declined until you complete the in-app questionnaire. 
If you mark that the payment was made by you, your card will be unfrozen — you can then retry the payment. If you don't recognise the payment, your card will be permanently terminated. You can order a new card to replace the terminated card.
If you accidentally terminate your card, you can reactivate it: Go to ‘Home’ on the bottom menu → tap the card icon in the top-right corner

---

[Article 2] Delayed (offline) card payments
## If you see a delayed transaction in-app
- Check previous transactions: review your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list to see if you previously made a payment to the same merchant. Sometimes, a merchant will retry a transaction that didn't work at a later time. If it's successful, you'll see both a reverted transaction and the successful transaction in-app
- Watch for duplicate charges: offline payments can sometimes cause duplicate transactions, and merchants may mistakenly charge you twice for a single transaction. To report unrecognised or incorrect charges, tap the payment in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and select 'Get help'

## If a delayed transaction causes your balance to go negative
Most likely, the negative balance is caused by the original transaction being in a pending state. The pending payment should be sent back to your account within a few hours or days and your balance should adjust.
You can check when the transaction is due to be sent back by tapping the transaction in your transaction historyhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list and checking its status.

---

[Article 3] Problem with a Direct Debit
## Insufficient funds for a Direct Debit
If you don’t have enough funds in the Direct Debit currency, we’ll check other currency balances in the order below, and take the full amount from the first one with enough funds. We can’t combine multiple currencies to complete the payment. We'll check your accounts for funds in this order:
- Transaction currency
- Your base currency
- Next active currency with enough balance
If no currency has enough funds in the order above, the Direct Debit will fail. You can customise your preferred currency for a specific Direct Debit in the 'Scheduled payments' section of the Revolut app.

## Direct Debit didn't trigger
There are a few reasons why a Direct Debit might not have been processed when you were expecting. It could have been previously deactivated, or the merchant may have scheduled it for a different date. To check if the Direct Debit is still active, and to verify its scheduled date:
- Go to 'Payments' on the bottom menu
- Tap the calendar icon in the top-right corner
- Choose the transaction to view the scheduled charge date
If you can't see the transfer, it was either disabled, or it wasn't successfully created in the first place. In these cases, contact the merchant directly to clarify the situation, or set it up again.

## Direct Debit failed or reverted
Check to see why your Direct Debit failed or was reverted in your transactions list. Just tap on the unsuccessful transaction for more info.

## Dispute a Direct Debit
Tap ‌the Direct Debit in your transactions list and choose the problem you're experiencing. You can then follow the prompts to dispute the Direct Debit.

---

[Article 4] My card payment is pending
A pending card payment happens when your transaction is authorised but hasn’t been fully processed yet. Typically, pending transactions are cleared within a few business days. 

## How to check the estimated reversal date
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Open your in-app transactionshttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the card payment
- In the 'Status' section, you'll see the reversal date

## Can I cancel a pending card payment?
We can't cancel pending payments on our end before the date mentioned in-app. We recommend allowing a few days for the merchant to take action.
The transaction amount is temporarily blocked in your account to fund the transaction and cannot be accessed by anyone until the merchant completes the payment, cancels the payment, or the transaction expires (in which case the funds will become available to you again).
If your transaction has been in a pending state for longer, contact the merchant directly, as only they can progress the transaction. If they take no action until the unblock date shown in the transaction status, the blocked funds should automatically be returned to you.

You'll find the estimated date of arrival for the transfer, along with guidelines in case you need additional support.

## Transfer is pending
When you make a transfer, it can take longer to be sent to your recipient in some situations. This is known as a pending transfer. Unfortunately, there's nothing we can do to speed up the process. Pending transfers can happen for several reasons:
- Being sent on a weekend or bank holiday when there's no processing
- Additional processing checks
- Additional information required
- Incorrect recipient details
- Currency or payment scheme used for transfer not supported by recipient or recipient bank
A transfer can be pending for up to 72 hours, but is usually completed in a few hours. If the transfer fails to reach the recipient, the transaction will be reverted. The funds should be available to you within 5 business days for domestic transfers and 15 business days for international transfers.

## Transfer is completed, but recipient hasn't received it
Sometimes, once a transfer is completed, recipient banks can take a bit longer (1-2 business days) to credit the funds to the recipient's account. 
If this time is exceeded, make sure you've entered the recipient's correct account details. If the details are correct, as the transfer has already left Revolut, you'll need to contact the recipient’s bank with the transfer confirmation.
You can download it after selecting your transfer in your transactions list. Next to 'Confirmation', tap 'Download'. 

## Transfer is reverted, but still hasn't reached my account
In the event of your outbound transfer being reverted but not reaching your account, you should request a return confirmation from the bank that reverted it. Most of the time, the recipient can request the document in PDF format from their bank.
It can take up to 5 business days for a domestic transfer and up to 15 business days for an international transfer to be returned to your Revolut account.

---

[Article 3] Why is my transfer to a bank account still pending?
When you make a transfer, it can take extra time to be sent to your recipient in some situations. This is known as a pending transfer, and it may occur for several reasons: 
- Being sent on a weekend or public holiday when there’s no processing
- Additional processing checks
- Additional information required
- Incorrect recipient details
- Currency or payment scheme used for transfer not being supported by recipient or recipient bank
You can track the status of your transfer by using the outbound transfer statushttps://app.revolut.com/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.why-is-my-transfer-still-pendingrevolut://app/form/outbound-transfer-status-under-quick-help-v-2?faqEntryPoint=question.why-is-my-transfer-still-pending tool in the Revolut app.

## When will the transfer go through?
A transfer may remain pending for up to 72 hours. In most cases, a transfer will eventually be marked as completed after a few hours.
If the transfer fails and doesn’t reach the recipient, the transaction will be reverted, and the funds should be available in your Revolut account promptly. To learn more about the potential reasons for a failed transfer, read this article/help/transfers/outbound-transfers/having-an-issue-with-sending-money/whys-my-transfer-been-declined.

---

[Article 4] Why is my incoming transfer pending?
A 'pending' status means the transfer is with us, but we need to complete some final checks before releasing the funds to your account. Common reasons for a pending transfer:
- We're waiting for required information from the sending bank
- The transfer needs to be reviewed to meet regulatory requirements
Pending transfers are usually processed within 3 business days. You should wait up to 5 business days before reporting the transfer as delayed or seeking support. If the transfer is still pending after this time, please contact our support team for help.

## My transfer was reverted
A 'reverted' status means we were unable to process the transfer, and the funds have been sent back to the sender's account. Common reasons for a reverted transfer:
- The transfer details were incorrect or incomplete (e.g. a missing reference number)
- We requested missing personal information for your Revolut account and didn't receive it in time

## What happens next?
The funds will automatically be returned to the sender, but the timeframe depends on the transfer type. Revolut cannot speed up this process as it depends on the sending bank and any intermediary banks.
- Domestic transfers: should arrive back in the sender's account within 5 business days
- International (SWIFT) transfers: can take up to 15 to 20 business days due to the multiple intermediary banks involved
The sender should contact their bank if the funds don't arrive within these timeframes. Once they receive the money, they can attempt to send you the transfer again using the correct details.

## Can I get an update on my case?
Not to worry, we'll update you during each stage of the dispute process and let you know the expected date for an outcome. We'll also provide an explanation when any decision has been reached via email and in-app.

## How to check the status of a dispute
In the Revolut mobile app: 
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu 
- Tap 'See all' to open your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- Tap the disputed transaction
- View the dispute tracker

## I need to provide updated information to Revolut
If we need any additional information to support your case, we'll let you know in-app. We'll wait up to a maximum of 15 days to hear back from you. If you fail to reply within this timeframe, we'll proceed to cancel your case.

## Chargeback timeframes
How long you'll likely need to wait for an outcome depends on the case type:
- Fraud cases: within 10 days after you report the matter to us
- ATM cases: up to 45 days after you report the matter to us
- Disputes: up to 120 days after you report the matter to us

---

[Article 2] A refund or chargeback was sent to my closed account
If you receive a refund or chargeback after closing your Revolut account, you can send us an email at support@revolut.com with the below information.

## Information to include
- A bank statement dated within the last 3 months with the alternate bank details you'd like us to send money to. It can be a photo, PDF file, or a screenshot, and your full name, address, and account number need to be visible
- A confirmation (screenshot, email, etc.) from the merchant showing that the refund was applied. It should contain the merchant's name, amount, and the last 4 digits of your Revolut card
Once we've verified the documentation, we'll initiate the transfer process to your desired alternate account. It typically takes between 3-5 business days for the refund or chargeback funds to arrive after we've processed it from our end.

---

[Article 3] Dispute a card payment
## Contact the merchant
Most issues like cancellations, refunds, or wrong charges are best resolved directly with the merchant. It’s faster and usually required before submitting a dispute. 
If you still need to dispute the transaction, make sure you gather evidence of the merchant's response, as this can significantly help your case.

## How to dispute a card transaction
If your direct contact with the merchant is unsuccessful, follow these steps to dispute the card transaction in your Revolut app:
- Tap the transaction in your transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. If you have multiple to report, you'll be able to add them in the form later
- Tap 'Get help'
- Select an issue
- Complete and submit the form
We'll review your case and get back to you with updates. You can also track the progress of your dispute in-app by tapping the disputed transaction on the transactions listhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list. Learn more about the disputes process and timeliness in this FAQ.

---

[Article 4] Dispute process and timelines
After you submit a dispute, we'll review your case and decide whether there's sufficient evidence to initiate a chargeback. A chargeback is a formal process to reverse the transaction through the card network (e.g. Visa, Mastercard).
This process is regulated by strict rules and evidence requirements. 
Up to 85% of disputes are resolved within 5 weeks, but complex cases may take up to 12 weeks. Since the process is regulated, we can't speed this process up. To track your dispute status, tap the transactionhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list in your Revolut app.

## Initial assessment
- You fill out a form to initiate the dispute
- Once the transaction is completed, we review your dispute case usually within 8 days, and if there is sufficient evidence, we will proceed to the next stage
- If we need more information, we'll reach out to you
- After this, we'll come to an initial decision. Depending on the type of dispute, we may be obliged to wait a minimum period of 15 days before raising with the merchant, as per card network rules
If your dispute is successful, you'll be refunded by us or the merchant. If your dispute is declined or cancelled, you're not refunded and your case is closed. If we raise your case to the merchant, your case is still open and proceeds to the next step. We'll keep you updated every step of the way.

## Merchant review
If we decide to raise the dispute to the merchant, we reach out to them. The merchant has 30 days to respond for Visa or 45 days for Mastercard transactions.
- If the merchant agrees or doesn't respond, you'll receive a refund for the associated transaction(s)
- If the merchant disagrees, they'll provide supporting documentation and the case will proceed to the next step

## Second assessment
We receive and review the merchant's evidence within 5 days and reach a decision. 
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
- If the evidence is insufficient, the dispute process continues

## Final assessment before resolution
Once the case is raised to the merchant for a second time (if needed), they have 30 days to respond, after which we'll make a final decision.
- If the merchant agrees, you'll receive a refund
- If the merchant provides sufficient evidence, the dispute is closed without a refund
If it's unresolved and the merchant decides to keep disputing, we may escalate the case to Visa or Mastercard for review, which may take approximately 40 additional days.

- Go to your Revolut Pro Home Page
- Select ‘More’

## To download statements after closing your account
- Tap your initial in the top-right corner 
- Select ‘Documents & Statements’
- Select ‘Pro’
- Tap ‘Account Statement’ and select the time period you want the statement for
- Follow the steps to generate the statement

---

[Article 2] How do I close my account?
Thinking of leaving? Switch to our Standard plan instead and keep your account. You'll still enjoy many features, with no monthly plan fees. If you still want to close your account, follow the steps below.

## Closure requirements
- All your accounts, Pockets, and currency accounts have a balance of zero
- You have no pending transactions. Check if you have a pending transaction by tapping this linkhttps://app.revolut.com/transactionsrevolut://app/transaction-list
- You have the latest version of the Revolut app installed
- You've downgraded your plan to Standard¹
- You've closed your investment account¹ 
- You have no active subscriptions or chargeback requests
- You have no active Kids & Teens accounts¹ 
- You have no active insurances through Revolut¹

## How to close your account in the app
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Account' and 'Close account'
Download and keep any statements you may need for future reference. We'll send you a confirmation of the closure once you've completed the process. 
For details on the data we retain after your account closure, read our Customer Privacy Noticehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/privacy/?geo-redirect=true.

## If your balance is too low for an outbound transfer
- Top up your account to meet the minimum amount required for outbound transfers
- Transfer the remaining funds to a Revolut friend via an internal transfer
- Donate the remaining funds to a worthy cause of your choice using our in-app Donations feature¹. To donate, you can use this link
If you want to close your account and no longer have access to your Revolut app, email us at support@revolut.com.
¹ For customers aged 18 or above only

---

[Article 3] Close my investment account
## To close your Investment account
- Go to 'Invest' on the bottom menu
- Select 'More' on the right side of the screen
- Tap 'Settings'
- Select ‘Close your brokerage account’
- Carefully review and agree to the 'Trading account closure' term

## Investment account closure process
- To close your account, follow the instructions above
- Your assets will be sold once you agree to the 'Trading Account Closure' terms
- Sale proceeds will be transferred to your designated account based on the instrument's trading currency 
- Your account will be closed once the balance reaches zero
You'll get statements for your closed account in an offboarding email, and can also request them via chat. If you've closed your investment account, and if you're still eligible, you can open a new account.
As selling assets in your investment account may trigger capital gains or losses, you should carefully consider the potential tax consequences of closing your investment account. Revolut doesn't advise on tax-related matters. If you need advice, we recommend contacting a financial advisor.

---

[Article 4] I want to close my Joint account
You can close a Joint account directly in-app, but first, you need to bring the account balance to zero. Either of the Joint account holders can withdraw money without needing the other's consent.

## How to close the Joint account
In the Revolut app:
- Go to 'Home' on the bottom menu
- Below your balance, tap Accounts
- Select your Joint account
- Tap 'Details'
- Scroll down and select 'Close Joint account'
Once you close the Joint account, we'll deactivate any cards linked to the Joint account. We'll also send ‌both co-holders their Joint account statements by email.

answer:

- You have the latest version of the Revolut app installed

## How to close your account in the app
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Account' and 'Close account'
Download and keep any statements you may need for future reference. We'll send you a confirmation of the closure once you've completed the process. 
For details on the data we retain after your account closure, read our Customer Privacy Noticehttps://www.revolut.com/legal/privacy/?geo-redirect=true.

## If your balance is too low for an outbound transfer
- Top up your account to meet the minimum amount required for outbound transfers
- Transfer the remaining funds to a Revolut friend via an internal transfer
- Donate the remaining funds to a worthy cause of your choice using our in-app Donations feature¹. To donate, you can use this link
If you want to close your account and no longer have access to your Revolut app, email us at support@revolut.com.
¹ For customers aged 18 or above only

---

[Article 2] How to close a Revolut Pro account
- Tap ‘Move’ to send your remaining balance to another account
- Go to your Revolut Pro Home Page
- Select ‘More’ 
- Select 'Details' and scroll down 
- Tap ‘Close Pro Account’ and confirm closure
If you have pending cashback or sales, wait until they have completed. You should be able to see the estimated completion date in-app.Then, move the money to your personal account and close the account using the steps above. 
If you’re not able to transfer your money from your Pro account to your personal account, contact our support team using the in-app chat. Once closed, you won’t be able to see your Pro account in the account switcher.

## To download statements after closing your account
- Tap your initial in the top-right corner 
- Select ‘Documents & Statements’
- Select ‘Pro’
- Tap ‘Account Statement’ and select the time period you want the statement for
- Follow the steps to generate the statement

[Article 3] Close my investment account
## To close your Investment account
- Go to 'Invest' on the bottom menu
- Select 'More' on the right side of the screen
- Tap 'Settings'
- Select ‘Close your brokerage account’
- Carefully review and agree to the 'Trading account closure' term

## Investment account closure process
- To close your account, follow the instructions above
- Your assets will be sold once you agree to the 'Trading Account Closure' terms
- Sale proceeds will be transferred to your designated account based on the instrument's trading currency 
- Your account will be closed once the balance reaches zero
You'll get statements for your closed account in an offboarding email, and can also request them via chat. If you've closed your investment account, and if you're still eligible, you can open a new account.
As selling assets in your investment account may trigger capital gains or losses, you should carefully consider the potential tax consequences of closing your investment account. Revolut doesn't advise on tax-related matters. If you need advice, we recommend contacting a financial advisor.

---

[Article 4] Change my address
## Your address is within the UK
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Account' and Personal details
- Tap 'Residential address' to edit it
Address changes to and from the territories listed below are considered as address changes within the same country, and you won't be required to close your account:
- Guernsey
- Isle of Man
- British Indian Ocean Territory
- Jersey

## Your address is not within the UK
If you're moving to a different country, you'll need to close your Revolut account to be able to open a new one in another supported country/help/profile-and-plan/profile-plan/verifying-identity/what-countries-are-supported.
- Tap your profile icon in the top-left corner
- Select 'Account' and Close account
- Follow the prompts required for the closure, including removing all balances and waiting for pending transactions to be completed
You can't have multiple accounts or keep your main account if you're no longer a resident in the country it was opened in. If your relocation is temporary, your account will still be usable. Learn more about account closure requirements in this FAQ/help/profile-and-plan/closing-account.

## Transferring your funds if you need to close the account
You can't transfer funds between your own Revolut accounts. Transfer the balance to an external account or to another Revolut customer before closing your account.
